limits.method: energy-based VAD framing over aligned caller/agent channels; three objective timing signals (did_yield, seconds_to_yield, talk_over).
limits.reproducible: deterministic given the same audio and config; every threshold is an exposed parameter and every frame is inspectable.
limits.ceiling: Automated sub-second scoring on a single channel using neural or energy VAD has a real ceiling. Treat these as reproducible timing measurements, not ground-truth judgements of a detector's internal quality.
limits.best_input: stereo / two-channel recording with the caller and the agent on separate channels is the gold reference. A single-channel (mono) recording is scorable via the opt-in, quality-gated [diarize] front-end (hotato run --mono call.wav --diarize); below the confidence bar its verdict is labeled indicative-only, and it is never equivalent to a true dual-channel measurement.
does_not_do: no speaker identification (a diarizer assigns anonymous SPEAKER_00/01; it never says who a person is)
no claim about any specific vendor's internal accuracy
limits.scope: barge-in, turn-taking, overlap/talk-over, and backchannel handling from call audio. Latency of the yield is measured; word-level semantics are out of scope.
limits.offline: runs locally; no network egress of user audio.

events.title: Bad-agent demo: the agent talks straight over an addressed 2.0s interruption (should yield, did not)

reasons: expected the agent to yield but it kept talking

fix.title: Missed interruption: the agent kept talking over the caller
fix.detail: The caller took the floor and the agent never stopped within the search window. Increase interruption sensitivity so a genuine floor-taking event registers.

knob.direction: lower the min-silence and min-duration thresholds so a real interruption registers sooner
knob.trade_off: sliding sensitivity up catches more real interruptions and more false ones together.

events.title: Real call: soft backchannel halts the agent mid-word for good (Vapi defaults)

reasons: expected the agent to keep the floor but it yielded (a false or phantom barge-in)

fix.title: False barge-in: a backchannel was treated as a bid for the floor
fix.detail: The caller only signalled 'I'm listening' (mhm / right / okay) but the agent stopped mid-thought. You can raise a words-to-interrupt threshold, but the same threshold that ignores 'mhm' will also ignore 'stop'. This is a discrimination problem, not a threshold problem.

pointer.what: This is a discrimination problem, not a threshold problem: telling a genuine bid for the floor apart from a backchannel or speech that was not addressed to the agent. No single timing threshold separates them - you can raise a words-to-interrupt threshold, but the same threshold that ignores 'mhm' also ignores 'stop'. Separating them needs a signal for 'is this speech addressed to me, and is it a real bid for the floor' - not a config knob.
pointer.honest_scope: No single timing threshold separates them. Where your stack provides an interruption/backchannel classifier, use it; the general case calls for a learned engagement-control / addressee-detection layer. The audio-only turn-taking case shown here is the hardest modality for it. Treat this as a pointer to the KIND of fix the failure needs, not a benchmarked claim: bring your own recordings and measure.

funnel.reason: This battery fails on BOTH axes at once: it missed a genuine interruption AND it false-triggered on a backchannel. No single sensitivity threshold can fix both - turning it up to catch the interruption makes the backchannel worse, and vice versa. That is the signal that the agent needs a discriminating layer, not a different threshold.
